old_claim.note: Bronze Casting Molds provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained because the node is broader than cast-bronze scale components.

source_shape.source_locator: Penn Museum balance-scale overview supports ancient weighing practices without making bronze casting molds a prerequisite for portable balances and weights.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Portable balances and standard weights require weighing standards and market/administrative context, not bronze casting molds as a direct prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Weights and balances can be stone, wood, clay, or metal; bronze molds are optional implementation detail, not the broad-node dependency.

ontology_before: Portable Balance Scales treated Bronze Casting Molds as an enabling prerequisite.
ontology_after: Portable Balance Scales remains tied to standardized weights/measures and market use, not bronze casting.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: Portable Balance Scales must not directly depend on Bronze Casting Molds unless narrowed to cast-bronze scale hardware.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to cast bronze balance hardware.
A source shows the earliest portable scales required bronze-cast components.
The scale node is split and this id is reserved for metal-cast scales only.
short_reason: Portable balances do not require bronze casting molds.
